name: Ratzelach (Pancakes from Poland)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour and salt in a large bowl.
2. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and pour in the milk, stirring from the center out to form a smooth batter.
3. Add the eggs and enclose them with a folding motion. The batter should be very thin.
4. Melt a small amount of fat in a medium-sized frying pan and grease the surface well.
5. When the pan is hot, pour just enough batter to cover the bottom, tiling it from side to side to spread the batter along the edges.
6. Brown on one side for about 1 minute, then flip and brown on the other side for another 1-2 minutes.
7. Stack the pancakes, sprinkling confectioners' or crushed sugar between each layer.
8. Cut into wedges for serving. Makes about 15 pancakes. Serves 2-3.
